name: Dynamic Field of View
description: Dynamic Field of View is a viewpoint behavior that frames action and communicates spatial information to the player.
design_notes: Dynamic Field of View should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For camera mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use dynamic field of view as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect dynamic field of view to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels oriented and confident because the game frames what matters.

parameters.name: follow_lag
parameters.description: How far behind the target the camera is allowed to feel.

parameters.name: framing_offset
parameters.description: Offset used to keep the important subject away from dead center.

parameters.name: transition_speed
parameters.description: How quickly the camera blends to the new behavior.

edge_cases: Dynamic Field of View is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Dynamic Field of View overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Dynamic Field of View runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Dynamic Field of View state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Dynamic Field of View plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Dynamic Field of View produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Dynamic Field of View strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Dynamic Field of View becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Dynamic Field of View with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Dynamic Field of View as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Dynamic Field of View with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Dynamic Field of View as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.
